fuzzy_prd:
Hey team, we need to get the embedded device build pipeline sorted out properly. Right now devs are manually copying engine binaries and plugin libraries around and it keeps going wrong on-device because things end up in the wrong place or the wrong variant gets picked. We talked about this in the last sprint retro and everyone agreed it's a time sink.

Basically we need a pipeline that takes a compiled app and its native plugins and produces whatever the device runtime actually needs — the right layout, the right files, signed and ready to install. There are a few different flavors depending on whether you're doing a managed or native app, and whether you want a final installable thing or a flat module you can embed into a host app. We also need it to handle that whole situation with prebuilt libraries where only some of them are valid for your target — like what we did for the compatibility filtering in that login SDK integration a while back, same idea but for arch and api version.

Also important: if someone tries to package without a signing cert set up, it should fail gracefully with a clear message rather than just blowing up. Each stage should report back exactly which files were produced so you can inspect what happened. Can someone pick this up? The JSON contract for the adapter is already roughed out somewhere in the test cases folder.

oracle_data.golden_api_signature: run_stage(stage: str, scenario: str | None) -> list[tuple[str, bool]] | BuildError

trigger_keywords: what files go in a debug asset bundle
which artifacts does the debug stage produce
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: The debug asset bundle stage must produce exactly three artifacts in this order: vm_snapshot_data, isolate_snapshot_data, and kernel_blob.bin. Each is reported as an artifact= line followed by a present=true|false line.

trigger_keywords: what does the C++ embedding stage produce
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: The native C++ embedding stage reports two artifacts: include/flutter.h (the staged public header) and libembedding_cpp.a (the compiled static archive), in that order, each with present=true.

trigger_keywords: when is the combined plugin object produced
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: A plugin declared as a static library is folded into the combined shared object lib/libflutter_plugins.so. A plugin declared as a shared library is NOT folded in — the combined object is absent (present=false) and instead the plugin's own shared object (lib/libsome_native_plugin.so) and any side-car shared objects (lib/libshared.so) are reported as present.

Plugin resources are copied recursively from the plugin's platform resource directory into a per-plugin sub-directory under res/ in the build output, preserving nested paths. For example a file at a/b.txt in a plugin named some_native_plugin appears at res/some_native_plugin/a/b.txt.

Only libraries valid for the target architecture and api version are copied: top-level shared objects (.so) are always copied; architecture-specific shared objects are copied only if the arch tag matches the target; api-version-specific shared objects are copied only if the api level is at or below the project's api level; top-level static archives (.a) are never copied. The user_libs= field lists the bare base names (stripping lib prefix and extension) of all selected libraries plus the toolchain default 'pthread', space-separated.

trigger_keywords: what .so does the managed tpk stage use
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: Managed-runtime stages (dotnet_tpk, dotnet_module) stage the embedder as lib/libflutter_tizen.so. Native stages (native_tpk, native_module) stage the embedder as lib/libflutter_tizen_common.so.

trigger_keywords: what happens when no signing cert
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: When the packaging stage (dotnet_tpk or native_tpk) is invoked without a configured signing certificate profile, the stage must not crash or emit artifact lines. It must emit exactly one line: error=no_certificate_profile

trigger_keywords: what does the packaging stage report
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: For a successful TPK packaging stage the output order is: artifact=tpk, artifact=res/flutter_assets, artifact=lib/libflutter_engine.so, artifact=lib/libflutter_tizen.so (or libflutter_tizen_common.so for native), artifact=res/icudtl.dat, artifact=res/flutter_assets/.app.deps.json, artifact=lib/libapp.so, artifact=lib/libflutter_plugins.so, artifact=lib/libshared.so — each followed by present=true.

trigger_keywords: what does add-to-app module stage emit
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: The dotnet_module stage emits in order: res/flutter_assets, lib/libflutter_engine.so, lib/libflutter_tizen.so, res/icudtl.dat, lib/libapp.so, src/GeneratedPluginRegistrant.cs, lib/libflutter_plugins.so — all present=true. Note: no tpk artifact and no .app.deps.json, unlike the tpk stage.

The native_module stage emits in order: res/flutter_assets, lib/libflutter_engine.so, lib/libflutter_tizen_common.so, res/icudtl.dat, lib/libapp.so, inc/generated_plugin_registrant.h, lib/libflutter_plugins.so, inc/flutter.h, Release/libembedding_cpp.a — all present=true. The native module includes the C++ embedding header and archive staged into inc/ and Release/ respectively, and uses a native generated registrant header rather than a .cs source file.

Each probed output is reported as two consecutive lines: artifact=<relative path> followed by present=true or present=false. Linkage metadata is reported as user_libs=<space-separated names>. An error condition is reported as a single line: error=<category>. There is no other output format — no JSON, no headers, no blank lines between pairs.

The user_libs= value is a space-separated list of bare library base names. The lib prefix and file extension are stripped (e.g. libshared_arm.so becomes shared_arm). The toolchain default pthread is always prepended as the first entry. The full order for the user_libraries scenario is: pthread some_native_plugin static shared shared_arm shared_40.

trigger_keywords: how should I organize the code
should I split into multiple files
should I separate business logic from IO
should business logic touch stdin stdout
how to separate parsing from logic
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: Scale-driven code organization: match physical structure to domain complexity. For micro-utilities or simple scripts a single well-organized file is fine. For systems with multiple distinct responsibilities (I/O routing, business rules, formatters, etc.) you MUST use a multi-file layout (e.g. src/, tests/) — never a single god file. Do not over-engineer simple problems, but strictly avoid monolithic files for complex domains. Strict separation of concerns is mandatory: core business logic must be completely decoupled from stdin/stdout and JSON parsing. The JSON test cases are a black-box contract for the execution adapter only — NOT the internal data model. Follow SOLID: SRP (separate parsing, routing, core execution, output formatting); OCP (core engine open for extension, closed for modification); DIP (high-level modules depend on abstractions, not I/O details). The public interface must be idiomatic. Errors must be modeled properly (typed exceptions or Result/Monad patterns), not generic faults.

trigger_keywords: how does the test harness call my code
what is the entry point for testing
how does the runner call the program
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: You must implement an execution adapter (dispatcher) that is logically and ideally physically separate from the core domain. It reads a single JSON object from stdin, inspects the 'action' field to route to the correct core function, then prints the result to stdout matching the per-feature output contract. All policy durations in inputs are in seconds; all time points are epoch milliseconds. The adapter must not contain any business logic — every numeric result and boolean decision is delegated to the core domain.

Deliver a bash test harness at rcb_tests/test.sh. It must accept --cases-dir <subdir> (default: test_cases) to point at a directory of *.json case files under rcb_tests/. For each case file it runs every case in the 'cases' array against the dispatcher, writing raw stdout (no PASS/FAIL metadata) to: rcb_tests/stdout/<cases-dir>/{filename_stem}@{case_index_zero_padded_3}.txt Example: rcb_tests/stdout/public_test_cases/feature1_next_retry_at@000.txt Output is namespaced by <cases-dir> so different directories never overwrite each other. A single entry point 'bash rcb_tests/test.sh' runs the full suite with the default dir.

trigger_keywords: can I see the test inputs
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: Yes, public test cases are provided, one file per feature. Ask me for a specific feature by name and I will give you the full test case JSON.

hidden_constraints.oracle_response:
Here are the public test cases for feature3_4_plugin_user_libraries:
{
  "description": "Native plugin compilation — user library selection and linkage metadata. A plugin ships a mix of prebuilt libraries under its platform lib directory: a top-level static archive, a top-level shared object, architecture-specific shared objects (one matching the target architecture, one not), and api-version-specific shared objects (one at or below the project's api level, one above). The stage copies only the libraries relevant to the target architecture and api version: top-level shared objects and matching arch/api shared objects are copied; the static archive and non-matching arch/api objects are skipped. It also records the selected user libraries (by bare base name, minus the `lib` prefix and extension) in a linkage metadata field. The report lists presence for each candidate library and a `user_libs=` line carrying the space-separated selected library names (which also includes the toolchain default `pthread`).",
  "cases": [
    {
      "input": {
        "stage": "native_plugins",
        "scenario": "user_libraries"
      },
      "expected_output": "artifact=lib/libstatic.a
present=false
artifact=lib/libshared.so
present=true
artifact=lib/libshared_arm.so
present=true
artifact=lib/libshared_x86.so
present=false
artifact=lib/libshared_40.so
present=true
artifact=lib/libshared_50.so
present=false
user_libs=pthread some_native_plugin static shared shared_arm shared_40
"
    }
  ]
}

feature1_debug_asset_bundle.json.description: Debug-mode asset bundling stage. Given a build environment whose kernel snapshot (app.dill) and the shared VM/isolate snapshot data have already been produced, the stage assembles the runtime asset bundle. The output is a line-oriented report listing each expected bundle entry as an `artifact=<relative path>` header followed by a `present=true|false` line indicating whether it was emitted under the bundle directory. For a debug build, the bundle must contain the two interpreter snapshot blobs plus the JIT kernel blob.

cases.expected_output:
artifact=vm_snapshot_data
present=true
artifact=isolate_snapshot_data
present=true
artifact=kernel_blob.bin
present=true

feature2_cpp_embedding.json.description: Native C++ embedding compilation stage. Given an embedding source tree that declares a static-library project and ships public C++ headers, the stage compiles the embedding into a static archive and stages the public headers into the build output. The report lists the staged header and the produced static archive, each as an `artifact=<relative path>` / `present=true|false` pair under the embedding output directory.

feature3_1_plugin_static_library.json.description: Native plugin compilation — static-library plugin. A project depends on one native plugin whose platform manifest declares a plugin class and a public header, and whose build descriptor marks it as a static library. The stage compiles the plugin and aggregates all static-library plugins into a single combined shared object. The report shows the plugin's public header is staged into the plugin include directory and that the combined plugin shared object is produced.

feature3_2_plugin_shared_library.json.description: Native plugin compilation — shared-library plugin. When a plugin's build descriptor marks it as a shared library (and ships its own shared object), the stage does NOT fold it into the combined static-plugin shared object; instead it emits the plugin's own shared object plus any side-car shared objects it provides. The report shows the combined aggregate object is absent while the plugin's individual shared object and its shipped shared object are present.

cases.expected_output:
artifact=lib/libflutter_plugins.so
present=false
artifact=lib/libsome_native_plugin.so
present=true
artifact=lib/libshared.so
present=true

feature3_3_plugin_resource_copy.json.description: Native plugin compilation — resource staging. A plugin that ships a resource tree under its platform resource directory has those resources copied recursively into a per-plugin resource sub-directory of the plugin build output, preserving nested paths. The report confirms a nested resource file is present at its mirrored path namespaced under the plugin's name.

feature3_4_plugin_user_libraries.json.description: Native plugin compilation — user library selection and linkage metadata. A plugin ships a mix of prebuilt libraries under its platform lib directory: a top-level static archive, a top-level shared object, architecture-specific shared objects (one matching the target architecture, one not), and api-version-specific shared objects (one at or below the project's api level, one above). The stage copies only the libraries relevant to the target architecture and api version: top-level shared objects and matching arch/api shared objects are copied; the static archive and non-matching arch/api objects are skipped. It also records the selected user libraries (by bare base name, minus the `lib` prefix and extension) in a linkage metadata field. The report lists presence for each candidate library and a `user_libs=` line carrying the space-separated selected library names (which also includes the toolchain default `pthread`).

cases.expected_output:
artifact=lib/libstatic.a
present=false
artifact=lib/libshared.so
present=true
artifact=lib/libshared_arm.so
present=true
artifact=lib/libshared_x86.so
present=false
artifact=lib/libshared_40.so
present=true
artifact=lib/libshared_50.so
present=false
user_libs=pthread some_native_plugin static shared shared_arm shared_40

feature4_1_managed_tpk.json.description: Managed-runtime application packaging into the platform package (TPK). Given a project that produces a managed-runtime application, the stage stages the engine runtime, the platform embedder shared object, the ICU data, the AOT app snapshot, the asset bundle (including the runtime dependency manifest), and the combined plugin objects into an ephemeral staging tree, then drives the platform packaging tool to produce the signed package and copies it to the output directory. Packaging requires a valid signing certificate profile: when one is configured the package is produced and the staged inputs are present; when none is configured the build is rejected with a normalized `error=no_certificate_profile` line. The `present=true` line under `artifact=tpk` indicates the final package was emitted.

cases.expected_output:
artifact=tpk
present=true
artifact=res/flutter_assets
present=true
artifact=lib/libflutter_engine.so
present=true
artifact=lib/libflutter_tizen.so
present=true
artifact=res/icudtl.dat
present=true
artifact=res/flutter_assets/.app.deps.json
present=true
artifact=lib/libapp.so
present=true
artifact=lib/libflutter_plugins.so
present=true
artifact=lib/libshared.so
present=true

feature4_2_native_tpk.json.description: Native application packaging into the platform package (TPK). Given a project that produces a native application (declaring a native build descriptor), the stage stages the engine runtime, the native-common platform embedder shared object, the ICU data, the AOT app snapshot, the asset bundle (including the runtime dependency manifest), and the combined plugin objects into an ephemeral staging tree, then builds and signs the package and copies it to the output directory. Packaging requires a valid signing certificate profile: when one is configured the package is produced and the staged inputs are present; when none is configured the build is rejected with a normalized `error=no_certificate_profile` line. Note the native package stages the native-common embedder object, distinguishing it from the managed-runtime package.

cases.expected_output:
artifact=tpk
present=true
artifact=res/flutter_assets
present=true
artifact=lib/libflutter_engine.so
present=true
artifact=lib/libflutter_tizen_common.so
present=true
artifact=res/icudtl.dat
present=true
artifact=res/flutter_assets/.app.deps.json
present=true
artifact=lib/libapp.so
present=true
artifact=lib/libflutter_plugins.so
present=true
artifact=lib/libshared.so
present=true

feature4_3_managed_module.json.description: Managed-runtime module packaging (add-to-app). Instead of producing a final package, the stage lays out the engine runtime, the platform embedder shared object, the ICU data, the AOT app snapshot, the generated managed plugin registrant source, the asset bundle, and the combined plugin objects directly into a flat output directory consumable by a host application. The report lists each emitted artifact under the output directory.

cases.expected_output:
artifact=res/flutter_assets
present=true
artifact=lib/libflutter_engine.so
present=true
artifact=lib/libflutter_tizen.so
present=true
artifact=res/icudtl.dat
present=true
artifact=lib/libapp.so
present=true
artifact=src/GeneratedPluginRegistrant.cs
present=true
artifact=lib/libflutter_plugins.so
present=true

feature4_4_native_module.json.description: Native module packaging (add-to-app). The stage lays out, into a flat output directory consumable by a host native application, the engine runtime, the native-common platform embedder shared object, the ICU data, the AOT app snapshot, the generated native plugin registrant header, the combined plugin objects, and the staged native embedding header and static archive. The report lists each emitted artifact under the output directory, including the embedding header staged into the include directory and the embedding archive staged into the configuration output directory.

cases.expected_output:
artifact=res/flutter_assets
present=true
artifact=lib/libflutter_engine.so
present=true
artifact=lib/libflutter_tizen_common.so
present=true
artifact=res/icudtl.dat
present=true
artifact=lib/libapp.so
present=true
artifact=inc/generated_plugin_registrant.h
present=true
artifact=lib/libflutter_plugins.so
present=true
artifact=inc/flutter.h
present=true
artifact=Release/libembedding_cpp.a
present=true
